name: Biagio's Spaghetti Carbonara
recipeIngredient: 500 g thin spaghetti, rotini or equivalent pasta
recipeInstructions:
1. Place a large bowl in the oven to warm at its lowest possible setting.
2. Soak chopped onion in cold water for 15 minutes to reduce pungency.
3. Chop pancetta or bacon into 5mm x 2cm strips.
4. Beat eggs and cream together with a fork, then add 50 g parmesan cheese to the mixture.
5. Wash pasta and bring water to boil. Add pasta when boiling begins; do not overcook.
6. Drain onions (squeeze out excess liquid) and sauté with pancetta or bacon in olive oil until onions are barely translucent.
7. Add wine and reduce heat until initial boiling ceases. Ensure meat remains tender, not crisp.
8. When pasta is cooked al dente, drain it but do not rinse; use immediately to prevent further cooking.
9. Remove the warmed bowl from the oven and add pasta. Toss with egg, cream, and cheese mixture so that residual heat gently cooks the eggs.
10. Add sautéed meat, onions, and wine (without excess fat) and toss until thoroughly combined.
11. Sprinkle remaining parmesan cheese to taste; toss again and serve immediately.
This recipe emphasizes clarity and consistency while maintaining the original's authentic preparation technique.
